dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7889 IH: Advancing Water Research and Collaboration Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Wittman (for himself, Ms. Brownley, Mr. Lawler, Mr. McGuire, Mr. Griffith, Mr. Hurd of Colorado, and Mr. McDowell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend the Water Resources Research Act of 1984 to reauthorize the water resources research and technology institutes program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Advancing Water Research and Collaboration Act of 2025 or the AWRC Act of 2025.
Section 103(4) of the Water Resources Research Act of 1984 (42 U.S.C. 10302(4)) is amended by inserting , including the growing artificial intelligence industry, after private industry.

section.header: Water resources research and technology institutes
section.text: Section 104 of the Water Resources Research Act of 1984 (42 U.S.C. 10303) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking subsection 104(g) of this Act and inserting subsection (g); and
by striking the subsection designation and all that follows through Any sums in paragraph (2) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2) and subject to subsection (g)(1), there is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $16,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2029.

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking (2) Research funds and inserting the following:

clause.text: in the third sentence, by striking Funds made and inserting the following:
by striking by institutes which focuses in the first sentence and all that follows through Such funds when appropriated in the second sentence and inserting the following:

quoted-block.text: by institutes with respect to any of the following:

subparagraph.text: Research that focuses on water problems and issues of a regional or interstate nature beyond those of concern only to a single State.
Research that relates to specific program priorities identified jointly by the Secretary and the institutes.
Research that relates to water problems identified by Congress as being of an interstate nature.

paragraph.text: Funds made available under this subsection

subparagraph.text: by striking the subsection designation and all that follows through 2025 in the first sentence of paragraph (1) and inserting the following:

subsection.header: Additional funds for research focused on water problems of interstate nature

paragraph.text: Of the amounts made available under subsection (f)(1) for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2029, 20 percent shall be used
